title: Condition-dependent stromal control of pancreatic cancer in a spatially grounded agent-based model: analysis and modeling code
description:
Analysis and modeling code accompanying the manuscript Condition-dependent stromal control of pancreatic cancer in a spatially grounded agent-based model (subtitle: food-medicine-homology compounds as a hypothesis-generating application).

The repository contains the spatial agent-based model (ABM) of the PDAC tumor–myCAF–immune ecosystem, the spatial-statistics pipeline for Xenium and CosMx data, and every figure-generation script. Included analyses: the confinement × immune-exclusion phase map with probabilistic phase uncertainty; global (Sobol) sensitivity analysis; a 30-seed progression-constrained Pareto evaluation of regimens; a Monte Carlo over compound effect, exposure weight, bioavailability and synergy (epistemic uncertainty); a CA19-9 observation model for the adaptive controller; patient-level spatial statistics; and spatial-necessity ablations including a well-mixed mean-field comparison. A full ODD-protocol specification of the ABM is provided in docs/manuscript/S3_ODD_protocol.md.

All analyzed datasets are public: Xenium PDAC (GEO GSE274673) and CosMx SMI PDAC (Mendeley Data doi:10.17632/kx6b69n3cb.1). In silico, hypothesis-generating study; outputs are testable hypotheses, not evidence of clinical efficacy or safety.
